name: EDR — CrowdStrike Falcon + SentinelOne
description: ATT&CK technique coverage for endpoint detection and response vendors CrowdStrike Falcon and SentinelOne. Coverage is derived from their NIST 800-53 control implementations (SI-3, SI-4, AU-2, CM-7, IR-4) mapped to ATT&CK techniques via CTID. Source: https://github.com/TeamStarWolf/TeamStarWolf/blob/main/data/vendor_to_technique.jsonl

techniques.comment: CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-3: NGAV + behavioral detection blocks/alerts on scripting interpreter abuse
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — PowerShell execution detection and blocking
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Windows Command Shell detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Unix Shell execution detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-3: process injection detection via kernel-level telemetry
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — DLL injection detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Portable Executable injection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Process Hollowing detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-3: ransomware behavioral detection (mass file encryption, shadow copy deletion)
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-3: malicious attachment/payload detection on endpoint after delivery
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — spearphishing attachment payload detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-4: network scanning activity detection from endpoint
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-4: anomalous credential use, impossible travel, first-seen login detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-4: account manipulation detection (new admin, MFA modification)
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — IR-4: post-exploitation detection and containment after public-facing app exploit
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-3: persistence mechanism detection (registry run keys, startup folder)
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-3: malicious service/process creation detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Windows Service creation detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-4: scheduled task creation by unusual processes
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Scheduled Task detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — SI-4: defense impairment detection (AV disable attempts, log clearing)
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — Disable or Modify Tools detection
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — IR-4: shadow copy deletion detection (pre-ransomware indicator)
CrowdStrike + SentinelOne — IR-4: service stop detection
